action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before, and you
have NO examples to start from. Your task is to work out how it works entirely on your
own, and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: bird, boat, hill, ship, river, lake, road, farmer, stone, king, dog, spear, tree, hunter
Adjectives: small, tall, dark, cold, big, red, blue, old
Verbs (write them in this base form): chase, find, hold, hear, feed, see, carry, watch
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three, four, five, six (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three, four, five, six) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
- zero or more adjectives may come before the noun.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a bird chase a boat
    six birds chase a boat
    a small bird chase a boat

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 5 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 4 English sentences (so at most
   20 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. You are starting from nothing, so spend your early probes establishing the
   basic words and word order, then test how number, case, definiteness, agreement and
   any sound changes work. Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 4 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I need to start by establishing the basic vocabulary and word order. I'll test simple sentences with different combinations of nouns, verbs, and articles to understand the structure. Let me begin with basic subject-verb-object patterns.

THOUGHTS: I'll start with simple sentences to establish: 1) basic word order (SVO), 2) how articles work, 3) how verbs are used, and 4) basic noun forms. I'll use common words from the list.

PROBES:
1. a bird chase a boat
2. the bird chase a boat
3. bird chase boat
4. a bird chase boat
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a bird chase a boat"  =>  vuma pato lopo
2. "the bird chase a boat"  =>  vuma na pato lopo
3. "bird chase boat"  =>  vuma pato lopo
4. "a bird chase boat"  =>  vuma pato lopo
STATUS: investigation round 1 of 5 done. Probes used: 4 of 20. 4 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the first round, I can see: 1) Word order is SVO (subject-verb-object), 2) "vuma" = bird, "pato" = chase, "lopo" = boat, 3) "na" appears before "bird" when "the" is used, suggesting "na" might be a definite article marker. Let me test more articles, numbers, and other nouns/verbs to build up the vocabulary.

PROBES:
1. the bird chase the boat
2. two birds chase a boat
3. a small bird chase a boat
4. the small bird chase a boat
action.label: response

probes: a small bird chase a boat
the small bird chase a boat

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the bird chase the boat"  =>  vuma na pato na lopo
2. "two birds chase a boat"  =>  vumavu nani pato lopo
3. "a small bird chase a boat"  =>  finu vuma pato lopo
4. "the small bird chase a boat"  =>  finu vuma na pato lopo
STATUS: investigation round 2 of 5 done. Probes used: 8 of 20. 3 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Excellent! Now I'm seeing clear patterns: 1) "na" is the definite article (before nouns), 2) "vumavu" = two birds (plural form), "nani" = two (number word), 3) "finu" = small (adjective), 4) word order is SVO with adjectives before nouns. Let me test more adjectives, other nouns, and plural forms to build vocabulary.

PROBES:
1. a big bird chase a boat
2. the big bird chase a boat
3. two big birds chase a boat
4. a dog chase a boat
action.label: response

probes: a big bird chase a boat
the big bird chase a boat
two big birds chase a boat

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a big bird chase a boat"  =>  mitani vuma pato lopo
2. "the big bird chase a boat"  =>  mitani vuma na pato lopo
3. "two big birds chase a boat"  =>  mitani vumavu nani pato lopo
4. "a dog chase a boat"  =>  felo pato lopo
STATUS: investigation round 3 of 5 done. Probes used: 12 of 20. 2 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Great progress! I now know: 1) "mitani" = big, 2) "felo" = dog, 3) plural is formed by adding "vu" suffix (vuma → vumavu), 4) number words come after the plural noun (nani after vumavu). Let me test more nouns, verbs, and other adjectives to complete the vocabulary.

PROBES:
1. a hunter chase a boat
2. the hunter chase a boat
3. a red bird chase a boat
4. the red bird chase a boat
action.label: response

probes: a red bird chase a boat
the red bird chase a boat

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a hunter chase a boat"  =>  pufi pato lopo
2. "the hunter chase a boat"  =>  pufi na pato lopo
3. "a red bird chase a boat"  =>  telulu vuma pato lopo
4. "the red bird chase a boat"  =>  telulu vuma na pato lopo
STATUS: investigation round 4 of 5 done. Probes used: 16 of 20. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I now have: "pufi" = hunter, "telulu" = red. I need to test more nouns and verbs, and also check if there are any other grammatical patterns. Let me test remaining nouns and some verbs to get a fuller picture before the exam.

PROBES:
1. a hunter chase a bird
2. the hunter chase a bird
3. a dog chase a bird
4. the dog chase a bird
action.label: response

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a hunter chase a bird"  =>  pufi vuma lopo
2. "the hunter chase a bird"  =>  pufi na vuma lopo
3. "a dog chase a bird"  =>  felo vuma lopo
4. "the dog chase a bird"  =>  felo na vuma lopo

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. the four hunters watch four small roads
2. the six red rivers carry two hills
3. five red hunters hear a old bird
4. four farmers see two hunters
5. a hunter chase two spears
6. the three small trees chase two spears
7. two big trees chase two hills
8. the six small farmers hold four cold lakes
9. five small trees chase three hills
10. two rivers see the three farmers

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: Let me analyze each sentence using the patterns I've discovered:
- Word order: SVO
- Definite article: "na" before nouns
- Plural: add "vu" suffix (vuma → vumavu)
- Adjectives before nouns
- Number words after the noun they modify

1. "the four hunters watch four small roads" → pufi nani pato na finu na finu
2. "the six red rivers carry two hills" → lopo nani telulu pato na nani na nani
3. "five red hunters hear a old bird" → pufi nani telulu nani pato finu vuma
4. "four farmers see two hunters" → nani nani pato nani pato
5. "a hunter chase two spears" → pufi pato nani na nani
6. "the three small trees chase two spears" → na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
7. "two big trees chase two hills" → na nani mitani na nani pato nani na nani
8. "the six small farmers hold four cold lakes" → nani nani finu na nani pato na nani na nani
9. "five small trees chase three hills" → na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
10. "two rivers see the three farmers" → lopo nani pato nani nani nani

ANSWERS:
1. pufi nani pato na finu na finu
2. lopo nani telulu pato na nani na nani
3. pufi nani telulu nani pato finu vuma
4. nani nani pato nani pato
5. pufi pato nani na nani
6. na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
7. na nani mitani na nani pato nani na nani
8. nani nani finu na nani pato na nani na nani
9. na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
10. lopo nani pato nani nani nani
action.label: response

answers: pufi nani pato na finu na finu
lopo nani telulu pato na nani na nani
pufi nani telulu nani pato finu vuma
na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
na nani mitani na nani pato nani na nani
nani nani finu na nani pato na nani na nani
na nani finu na nani pato nani na nani
lopo nani pato nani nani nani
targets: pufivu pino na finu vuvuvu pino lulipo
telulu navuvu tafu na pupovu nani fuma
telulu pufivu pi nali vuma mape
finu tufevu veve na vativu nani lopo
mitani tufevu nani pupovu nani lopo
finu vivavu tafu na lavutu linofovu pino tane
finu tufevu pi pupovu veve lopo
navuvu nani vivavu veve na fovu
